methods.description: Process for preparing seawater with specific nutrient concentrations and removing trace metals for experimental use.

steps.description: Seawater used for the experiment (salinity 34.2) was collected from a coastal region in the North Pacific, near Onjuku, Chiba, Japan.
About 50 l of seawater was filtered through a 0.22-mm cartridge filter.
Stock solutions of macronutrients were passed through a Chelex 100 resin to eliminate trace metals.
steps.searchable_summary: remove trace metals using Chelex 100 resin

steps.description: Add macronutrients to the filtered seawater to make final concentrations of 100 mmol l 21 NO 3 2 , 6 mmol l 21 PO 4 2 , and 150 mmol l 21 Si(OH) 4.
steps.searchable_summary: enrich seawater with specific nutrient concentrations

steps.description: The seawater with added nutrients was aged for more than 1 month in an acid-washed 50-l polypropylene carboy.
steps.searchable_summary: age nutrient-enriched seawater for over a month

steps.description: The aged seawater was passed through a 0.1-mm cartridge filter to sterilize it and to eliminate particulate iron before use.
steps.searchable_summary: sterilize and remove particulate iron from aged seawater

methods.description: Setup for culturing T. weissflogii in modified Aquil medium under controlled light and temperature conditions.

steps.description: An algal strain of T. weissflogii was maintained in modified Aquil medium at 208C under cool fluorescent light at 150 mmol photons m 22 s 21.
steps.searchable_summary: culture T. weissflogii in modified Aquil medium under specified conditions

steps.description: All equipment used in the culture experiment was acid-washed followed by thorough rinsing with Milli-Q water.
steps.searchable_summary: acid-wash and rinse equipment for culture experiment

steps.description: Preparation and sampling were conducted in a class 1000 clean room and at a class 100 clean bench, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct preparation and sampling in clean room and bench

methods.description: Procedures for monitoring growth and analyzing cellular components of T. weissflogii.

steps.description: Growth was monitored daily at a fixed time using a Multisizer 4 Coulter Counter to calculate specific growth rates and to measure cell volume and surface area.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor growth and measure cell volume using Coulter Counter

steps.description: Specific growth rates were determined from the slope of a plot of the natural log density of biovolume against time during the exponential growth phase.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate specific growth rates from biovolume data

steps.description: At the end of the experiment, cells were harvested on precombusted GF/F filters for particulate organic C, particulate N, and particulate P analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest cells for particulate organic analysis

steps.description: For biogenic Si analysis, samples were collected on a 0.8-mm polycarbonate filter.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples for biogenic Si analysis

steps.description: For POC and PN analysis, filters were freeze-dried, and the concentrations were measured using a CHN analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: measure POC and PN concentrations using CHN analyser

steps.description: Filters for PP analysis were combusted in high temperature followed by acid hydrolysis and measured using a spectrophotometer.
For BSi analysis, the filter was digested in 0.5% Na2CO3 solution and measured with a QuAAtro-2 continuous flow analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: measure BSi concentrations using QuAAtro-2 analyser
